folio.label: 110v
folio.l: Neditú it nomore , for hi~ to go ne ride
He was a poynted , there he wold a byde
Placebo cam , and eke hise frendes sone
And alderfirst , he bad he~ alle a boone
That noone of he~ , noone argumentez make
A geyn the purpos , whicú æ he hatú take
Whicú purpos , was plesyng to god , seide he
And verray ground , of his prosperite
He seide there was , a mayden in the toun
Whicú that of beaute , had gret renoun
Al were it so , she were of smal degree
Suffisetú hym , hire ©outhe and hire beaute
Whicú maide he seide , he wolde han to his wyf
To lede in ease and holynesse , his lyf
And thanke god , that he mygút haue hire al
That no wygút , his blisse parten shal
And preyed hem , to laboure in this nede
And shape that he , faille nat to spede
For than he seide , his spirit was at ease
Than is q¶ he , no thyngö may me displese
Saue o thyngö , prykketú in my conscience
The whicú I wol reherce , in ©oure päsence
I haue q¶ he herd seid , ful ©ore a go
There may noman haue , ¹fyt blisses two
This is to seyn , in erthe and eke in heuene
For thougú he kepe hi~ , fro the synnes seuene
And eke from euäy braunche , of thilke tree
ñet is there , so parfyt felicite
And so grete ese , and lust in mariage
That euäe I am a gast , now in myn age
That I shal lede now , so mery a lyf
So delicat , witú outen woo and strif
That I shal haue , myn heuene in erthe heere
For sithe that verray heuene , is bougút so dere
Witú tribulaciou~s , and gret penaunce
How shuld I than , that lyue in swicú plesaunce
As alle wedded men , don witú here wyues
Come to the blisse , there crist eterne , oon lyue ys
This is my drede , and ©e myne bretheryn twey
Assoyletú me , this questiou~ , I prey
Iustinus , whicú that hated his foly
Answered a noon , rigút in his iapery
And for he wold , his longe tale a bregge
He wold noon auctorite , allege
But seide sire , so there be noon obstacle
